risk_if_not_implemented: Without Test Data Integrity, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Data Protection
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to facilitate the implementation of data protection controls.
compensating_control_1.justification: Data Protection (DCH-01) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Test Data Integrity (TDA-10.1) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: De-Identification (Anonymization)
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to anonymize data by removing Personal Data (PD) from datasets.
compensating_control_2.justification: De-Identification (Anonymization) (DCH-23) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Test Data Integrity (TDA-10.1) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
